conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who is 5, Blake who is 21, and Charlie who is 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you assess eligibility for Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13).

Let me start by asking: Are you responsible for each of these children? By this I mean, do they live with you and are you the person who is mainly responsible for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me and I'm responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now, regarding Blake who is 21 - is Blake currently in approved education or training? This would typically be full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels or equivalent) or approved training programs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Now I need to ask about your immigration status and residency: Are you or your partner (if you have one) currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here? For example, are you a UK citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that allows you to live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Does anyone else also have the children living with them, or is there anyone else who might be claiming Child Benefit for any of Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. They all live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, here's the outcome for your Child Benefit eligibility:

**Alex (age 5): Eligible ✓**
Alex is under 16, lives with you, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Blake (age 21): Not Eligible ✗**
Unfortunately, Blake is not eligible. Child Benefit can only be claimed for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is 21 and not in education or training, they don't meet the age criteria.

**Charlie (age 13): Eligible ✓**
Charlie is under 16, lives with you, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.

**Summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake. You would receive payments for two children.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit claim?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with the claimant who is responsible for them. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training). Alex meets all the criteria for eligibility.

Blake is 21 years old and is not in any approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can only be claimed for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is over 20, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of education status.

Charlie is 13 years old, which is under 16. Charlie lives with the claimant who is responsible for them. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. According to the eligibility rules, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training). Charlie meets all the criteria for eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with the right to reside and is responsible for three children: Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13). All children live with the claimant full-time and no one else is claiming for them. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility exists for children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Alex and Charlie are both under 16 and therefore eligible. Blake is 21 and not in education or training, so exceeds the age limit and is not eligible.
